title: Human Resources (HR) Department
learning_objectives: Understand the role of the Human Resources Department.
Learn the employee lifecycle from recruitment to retirement.
Understand HR responsibilities in training, performance and employee welfare.
Recognize how HR supports organizational success.
Understand the use of SAP HCM in HR management.

introduction.title: What is the Human Resources Department?
introduction.content: The Human Resources (HR) Department is responsible for managing the organization's workforce. It oversees recruitment, onboarding, training, attendance, payroll coordination, performance management, employee engagement and compliance with labor laws. HR ensures that the organization has skilled, motivated and productive employees.

business_purpose.title: Why is HR Important?
business_purpose.content: Employees are the foundation of every organization. HR ensures that the right people are hired, developed and retained while maintaining a positive work environment. Effective HR practices improve productivity, employee satisfaction and long-term organizational growth.

key_responsibilities.description: Identify hiring needs, advertise vacancies and conduct interviews.
Help new employees integrate into the organization.
Organize technical, safety and professional training programs.
Evaluate employee performance and support career development.
Resolve workplace concerns and maintain a positive work culture.
Maintain employee records, attendance and policy documentation.

key_hr_concepts.description: The process of attracting and selecting suitable candidates.
Helping new employees become productive members of the organization.
Evaluating employee performance and setting development goals.
Building motivation, commitment and job satisfaction.

coordination_with_departments.purpose: Provides trained operators and production staff.
Supports payroll processing and employee benefits.
Organizes mandatory safety training and awareness programs.
Creates employee system accounts and access permissions.
Maintains employee master data and HR processes.

business_example.title: HR Operations at JSL
business_example.content: A new Graduate Engineer Trainee joins JSL. HR completes document verification, issues an employee ID, coordinates SAP HCM registration and schedules induction training. The employee attends safety training, department orientation and technical sessions before beginning work in the assigned department.

important_notes: Employees are the organization's most valuable resource.
Continuous learning improves both employee performance and business success.
HR policies should be applied fairly and consistently.
A positive work environment increases employee engagement and productivity.
